scope: Defines the source repair plan for terminal_event rows whose terminal pricing is blocked by source degradation or declared-vs-actual last_tradable_date mismatch. This contract codifies repair requirements, required flags, and prohibitions. It does not implement any repair logic, produce prices, or unblock rows.

references.terminal_last_tradable_close_audit: audit of 10 terminal_event_unpriced rows: 4 degraded source with auditable bars, 6 declared last_tradable_date suspended with actual earlier tradable close

degraded_terminal_source_with_auditable_.description: bars_daily close, adj_factor, and volume exist and are auditable for the last tradable date, but the terminal_event_daily shared source carries a contract_degraded flag per run_input_contract. The terminal event (e.g. delist) is recorded but the shared source is not trusted for direct pricing. The row remains a hard blocker until source repair is completed.
degraded_terminal_source_with_auditable_.audit_finding: 4 rows have auditable bars_daily close/adj_factor/volume but terminal_event source is contract_degraded

repair_requirements.source_credibility_upgrade_condition: terminal_event source must be upgraded from contract_degraded to auditable by either (a) cross-validating terminal_event_daily fields against bars_daily and tradability_daily for the same instrument-date pairs, or (b) sourcing a non-degraded terminal_event record from an alternative auditable source. The upgrade decision must be recorded with a verifiable audit trail.
repair_requirements.bars_daily_as_pricing_input_condition: bars_daily close and adj_factor may serve as pricing input for last_tradable_close only when (a) the trading date is confirmed as the last tradable date by tradability_daily, (b) close > 0 and adj_factor > 0 are verified, (c) volume > 0 confirms actual trading activity on that date, and (d) the terminal_event_source_degraded_flag is explicitly recorded and retained.
repair_requirements.entry_to_terminal_priced_last_tradable_c: Only after source repair is completed and the repair audit is verified may the row enter the terminal_priced_last_tradable_close path. The repair must produce a repaired_terminal_event_daily or terminal_event_repair_audit record before any pricing is attempted.

prohibited_actions: Unblock the row without completing source repair audit
Price the row using bars_daily before source credibility is upgraded
Drop the terminal_event_source_degraded_flag before repair is verified
Enter terminal_priced_last_tradable_close without repaired terminal event source

declared_last_tradable_date_suspended.description: The terminal_event declared last_tradable_date falls on a suspension/non-trading day. The actual last trading day with confirmed close/adj_factor/volume precedes the declared date. The declared last_tradable_date is the suspension date; the actual last tradable date must be inferred from tradability/bars data. The row remains a hard blocker until the date discrepancy is resolved and recorded.
declared_last_tradable_date_suspended.audit_finding: 6 rows have declared last_tradable_date equal to suspension date, actual last trading day is earlier with auditable prices

repair_requirements.infer_actual_last_tradable_date_from_tra: actual_last_tradable_date must be inferred by scanning backward from the declared last_tradable_date through tradability_daily and bars_daily to find the most recent trading day with (a) tradability_daily confirms the date is tradable, (b) bars_daily close > 0 and adj_factor > 0, and (c) bars_daily volume > 0. The inferred date must be the latest date satisfying all three conditions and must precede or equal the declared last_tradable_date.
repair_requirements.entry_to_terminal_priced_last_tradable_c: Only after (a) actual_last_tradable_date is inferred and verified, (b) the declared-vs-actual date difference is recorded with all required fields, and (c) terminal_exit_approximation_flag or source_repair_flag is set, may the row enter the terminal_priced_last_tradable_close path using the actual last tradable date close/adj_factor.

prohibited_actions: Use declared last_tradable_date as actual last_tradable_date without verification
Price the row without confirming actual last tradable date has close/adj_factor/volume
Enter terminal_priced_last_tradable_close without terminal_exit_approximation_flag or source_repair_flag

global_prohibitions.portfolio_must_not_repair_terminal_event: The portfolio layer must never repair, infer, or supplement terminal_event source fields. All source repair must occur upstream in the execution path or a dedicated repair audit step.
global_prohibitions.build_project_panels_must_not_invent_pri: build_project_panels.py must not invent, approximate, or backfill terminal exit prices. Its role is passthrough or standardization only.
global_prohibitions.zero_recovery_prohibition_detail: zero_recovery is not admitted as a repair-plan outcome. Rows that cannot be repaired through source repair must remain hard_blocker=true and must not silently fall to zero_recovery.

recommended_implementation_order.action: Implement source repair audit (no price output)
recommended_implementation_order.description: Build the repair audit logic that (a) cross-validates terminal_event_daily against bars_daily and tradability_daily for degraded source rows, and (b) infers actual_last_tradable_date for suspended-date rows. The audit outputs a terminal_event_repair_audit record with all required flags and date differences, but does NOT produce actual_exit_date or actual_sell_price.
recommended_implementation_order.rationale: Repair audit must precede pricing to ensure all source credibility decisions and date corrections are verified before any price is computed.

recommended_implementation_order.description: Produce a repaired_terminal_event_daily view or terminal_event_repair_audit table that carries the corrected last_tradable_date, required flags (terminal_event_source_degraded_flag, terminal_exit_approximation_flag, source_repair_flag), and the declared-vs-actual date difference record.
recommended_implementation_order.rationale: The repaired output is the single source of truth for downstream pricing. It separates repair concerns from pricing concerns.

recommended_implementation_order.action: Wire execution path upstream to optionally produce terminal_priced_last_tradable_close from repaired source
recommended_implementation_order.description: Allow the upstream execution path to consume the repaired_terminal_event_daily or terminal_event_repair_audit output and, if the repair flags permit, apply the last_tradable_close pricing layer to produce terminal_priced_last_tradable_close with actual_exit_date, actual_sell_price, and execution_delayed_realized_return.
recommended_implementation_order.rationale: Pricing is a separate decision from repair. The execution path decides whether a repaired row qualifies for last_tradable_close pricing.

recommended_implementation_order.action: Restore portfolio dry-run after all resolvable blockers are cleared
recommended_implementation_order.description: Re-enable portfolio dry-run only when zero rows with actual_exit_date=NULL remain in the backtest_executable subset. Portfolio must still fail-fast on any residual hard blockers.
recommended_implementation_order.rationale: Portfolio dry-run is the final verification that all exit paths are fully resolved. It must only be restored after repair and pricing are complete.

notes: This contract defines repair requirements only. It does not implement repair logic, produce prices, or unblock rows.
Both repair cases default to still_hard_blocker=true. They can only transition to terminal_priced_last_tradable_close after repair audit is completed and verified.
zero_recovery is not an admissible outcome of this repair plan. Rows that cannot be repaired remain hard_blocker=true.
planned_exit_date must never substitute for actual_exit_date under any circumstance.
Portfolio layer must never perform source repair. All repair work must occur upstream.
build_project_panels.py must not invent or approximate prices for terminal exits.
The trainer/model_scores layer must not handle terminal event resolution.
This repair plan is a prerequisite for terminal_priced_last_tradable_close, not a replacement for it.
